data.title: Disney cancels $1B investment in OpenAI; D'Amaro expected to assess Epic Games cuts
data.description:
Media conglomerate Disney (DIS) is cancelling its $1B investment in OpenAI after the ChatGPT owner announced Wednesday that it will shutter its AI-powered short video generation app Sora, according to multiple media reports.

The deal, which included a licensing pact for over 200 Disney characters for the Sora app, was supposed to be a gateway for OpenAI to tap into valuable Hollywood intellectual property amid the film industry's fight against AI startups that violate copyrighted content. 

"As the nascent AI field advances rapidly, we respect OpenAI’s decision to exit the video generation business and to shift its priorities elsewhere," according to a Disney spokesperson. "We appreciate the constructive collaboration between our teams and what we learned from it, and we will continue to engage with AI platforms to find new ways to meet fans where they are while responsibly embracing new technologies that respect IP and the rights of creators."

The news also comes about a week into new Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro's tenure.

Separately, D'Amaro is expected to evaluate Disney's exposure in Epic Games, the company behind the popular Fortnite game, after it announced job cuts on Tuesday due to slowing user engagement.

Disney made a $1.5B investment back in February 2024 to take a 9% stake in Epic Games; D'Amaro spearheaded that deal under previous CEO Bob Iger.

data.title: AI presents most significant catalyst cybersecurity industry has experienced in 20 years: Wedbush
data.description:
The rise of artificial intelligence has created the most significant catalyst the cybersecurity industry has experienced in two decades, according to Wedbush.

The financial firm finds industry stalwarts such as CrowdStrike (CRWD), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Zscaler (ZS), Check Point (CHKP), and Rubrik (RBRK) stand out as the most likely winners of the AI windfall over the coming years.

"We note that today we estimate cyber security represents roughly 5% of IT budgets and will likely be doubling over the next few years to 10% as protecting the tidal wave of agents, AI use cases, and broader technology push poses massive risks to many organizations we spoke with on the floor," said Wedbush analysts following their discussions at the RSA Conference this week in San Francisco. "In essence, AI makes cyber security even a higher priority but, importantly, will attach this technology to many strategic AI deployments, which will translate into hundreds of billions of dollars incrementally coming into the industry over the coming years."

AI startups OpenAI (OPENAI) and Anthropic (ANTHRO) have released their own cybersecurity agents recently, such as Codex Security and Claude Code Security, respectively. However, Wedbush does not believe these types of developments will diminish the need for cybersecurity vendors.

"The increased use of AI has dramatically lowered the cost, skill, and time required to execute sophisticated attacks while massively elevating their scale/precision," Wedbush added. "AI agents and autonomous workflows dramatically increase the attack surface further, with more APIs, more machine identities, more lateral movement risk, and more cloud-native workloads creating entirely new vectors that legacy point solutions were never designed to address. Critically, AI does not reduce the need for endpoint, identity, cloud, and SOC automation; it multiplies it as more enterprises deploy LLM-powered agents….cyber becomes the enforcement layer of AI, not a casualty of it."

Most cybersecurity stocks were moving higher on Wednesday. Check Point had inched up 0.8%, CrowdStrike had ticked up 1%, Palo Alto had increased 1.5%, and Zscaler had edged up 3%. SentinelOne (S) had popped up 5%.

data.title: Canada discussing potential Keystone XL revival with Trump administration officials
data.description:
Canadian officials spoke to Trump administration representatives about a proposed revival of part of the canceled Keystone XL oil pipeline at a meeting in Houston this week, Canada's Natural Resources Minister Tim Hodgson said Tuesday, according to Reuters.

The project proposed by pipeline company South Bow (SOBO) and ‌U.S. partner Bridger Pipeline was one of the ‌topics Hodgson said he and Canada's Ambassador to the U.S., Mark Wiseman, discussed with U.S. Energy Secretary Wright and U.S. Interior Secretary Burgum.

Canada is positioning the potential of a new cross-border oil pipeline as a way it ‌can help the U.S. achieve energy security, Hodgson ⁠said in an interview at the ‌CERAWeek by S&P Global conference.

The U.S. is the world's largest producer of oil at 12M-13M bbl/day, "but they consume 20M," Hodgson said, "and they understand that Canada provides ~63% of that difference."

Hodgson said he also made it clear ‌at this week's meeting that Canada is aggressively working to expand its oil exports to non-U.S. markets by completing a planned 300K bbl/day expansion of the Trans Mountain pipeline that runs from ⁠Alberta to the Pacific Coast.

The Keystone XL project, ​which was canceled by former President Biden, is fully permitted ⁠on the Canadian side, but a presidential permit would be needed for the pipeline to cross the Canada-U.S. border, and state regulatory permits also would be needed.

"The President's entire energy team has been working diligently with our partners ​in Canada to work through the permitting process," an unnamed Trump administration told Reuters.

data.title: Edap targets $50M–$54M HIFU revenue for 2026 with continued U.S. and international expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: EDAP TMS S.A. (EDAP) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ryan Rhodes described 2025 as a transformative year, highlighting "39% revenue growth in our core HIFU business and record commercial performance for Focal One." Rhodes indicated that much of the growth was driven by accelerated adoption in the U.S., citing "record system placements and strong procedure growth." He emphasized that "the fourth quarter was the strongest quarter in the company's history for HIFU revenue, representing an increase of 34% over the same period last year."
  • Rhodes detailed commercial achievements, including a record 15 Focal One placements in Q4—14 of which were cash sales—with 10 in the U.S., the highest quarterly total to date. He noted expanding adoption among leading academic centers and community hospitals, including placements at Aurora St. Luke's Medical Center in Wisconsin and four new systems in Pennsylvania, as well as conversions from legacy HIFU technology at major institutions.
  • International expansion was marked by first-time sales in India and Argentina, with additional system sales in Italy and Spain, further strengthening the company's global footprint. Rhodes noted, "our Focal One capital sales momentum also continues to expand in existing regions as well as new emerging markets."
  • Regulatory progress included FDA clearance for the latest Focal One Robotic HIFU platform with advanced ultrasound imaging, supporting future AI-driven developments. Rhodes highlighted new reimbursement rates, stating "a national facility payment average of $9,671, representing a 4.6% increase versus 2025."
  • On clinical expansion, Rhodes pointed to ongoing studies in endometriosis and BPH, including a new clinical trial in South America in collaboration with Mount Sinai Health System.
  • CFO Ken Mobeck reported, "HIFU revenue for the full year 2025 was EUR 33.1 million, an increase of 39% as compared to HIFU revenue of EUR 23.8 million for the full year 2024." Mobeck explained the total revenue decrease was due to a "27% decline in our noncore distribution and ESWL businesses, which offset the 39% growth in core HIFU business."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated 2026 guidance, stating, "we expect core HIFU revenue to be in the range of USD 50 million to USD 54 million, representing 34% to 45% growth over 2025." Combined noncore revenue was guided to "USD 22 million to USD 26 million."
  • Rhodes explained, "The combination of these priorities, commercial execution, indication expansion and continued technology innovation and leadership underpins our confidence in our 2026 outlook and beyond."

Financial Results

  • HIFU revenue in Q4 2025 was EUR 11.7 million, a 34% increase from EUR 8.8 million in Q4 2024. Total revenue for the quarter was EUR 18.9 million, down 7% from EUR 20.3 million in Q4 2024, due to a 38% decline in noncore segments.
  • Gross margin for the quarter was EUR 8.1 million, compared to EUR 9.1 million in Q4 2024. Gross margin on net sales was 42.6%, down from 44.8%, impacted by tariffs and inventory reserves.
  • Operating expenses were EUR 13.2 million versus EUR 12.8 million in Q4 2024, primarily from increased HIFU investments. Operating loss was EUR 5.2 million compared to EUR 3.7 million, and net loss was EUR 8.2 million or EUR 0.22 per share, compared to EUR 1.9 million or EUR 0.05 per share last year. The increase was attributed to "a EUR 2.5 million noncash charge related to warrants and interest expense" and "a EUR 2 million negative currency impact."
  • Inventory decreased to EUR 10.9 million from EUR 13.8 million at the end of Q3. Total cash and cash equivalents rose to EUR 17.4 million from EUR 10.6 million, reflecting the EIB Tranche A drawdown.

Q&A

  • Michael Sarcone, Jefferies: asked for details on procedure versus capital sales growth and Q1 trends. Ryan Rhodes responded, "Procedure growth, again, we saw a notable increase Q4, 28% over prior year...we look outward and see a strong year for us, both in terms of capital sales as well as procedure growth." Mobeck added, "With our installed base now at 165 units, that's also going to lead to disposable sales growth and service growth as well."
  • Sarcone asked about Q1 procedure trends and storm impacts. Rhodes answered, "No impact that I can point to. I would say, generally, we see a nice ramp developing. ... Q1 is ramping as planned."
  • Joseph Downing, Piper Sandler: questioned HIFU revenue seasonality and noncore wind-down. Mobeck confirmed, "Q4 to be the highest growth quarter...the lowest quarter will be in Q3. ... ESWL is roughly 20% to 25% of the noncore."
  • Swayampakula Ramakanth, H.C. Wainwright: asked about margin impacts from tariffs and strategy. Mobeck said, "We have budgeted about $2.5 million in tariffs in 2026 to be conservative, and we're just going to continually monitor what everything is happening from the government regarding those."
  • Ramakanth also asked about cash sales and pricing strategy. Rhodes replied, "cash sales will be our lead theme going forward...We have a price increase that went in place last year. We haven't changed our pricing strategy at the beginning of this year, and we see our average selling prices tend to hold or even slightly increase."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the sustainability of growth in procedures and capital sales, seasonality in revenue, the impact of tariffs, and the transition of revenue mix, with a neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought clarifications on guidance and operational levers.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing commercial momentum, strategic execution, and guidance reiteration. Rhodes stated, "we are confident in our ability to deliver sustainable growth and create long-term shareholder value."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remained consistent, while management demonstrated increased confidence, particularly citing record performance and strong 2026 guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reiterated and raised its focus on HIFU growth, with explicit 2026 guidance for core HIFU revenue and noncore segment management. Strategic emphasis shifted even more toward capital sales and recurring procedure revenues, whereas previously, there was more discussion of clinical validation and market access progress.
  • Key metrics saw HIFU revenue growth rates increase both year-over-year and sequentially, while noncore revenue continued to decline as planned.
  • Management's confidence heightened, with stronger language around commercial execution and market penetration compared to the previous quarter.
  • Analysts’ questions in both quarters centered on growth drivers, reimbursement, and operational risks, but recent focus increased on the sustainability of cash sales and pricing stability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged exposure to tariffs on finished goods shipped from France to the U.S., budgeting $2.5 million for 2026 and noting ongoing monitoring of government policy.
  • The decline in noncore distribution and ESWL businesses was cited as a known risk, with strategy to manage and potentially exit less accretive agreements.
  • Currency impacts and noncash charges below the operating line contributed to increased net loss, as noted by the CFO.
  • Analysts inquired about the duration and materiality of noncore revenue and the potential for further margin pressure from tariffs.

Final Takeaway

EDAP TMS S.A. closed 2025 with record HIFU revenue growth and robust commercial momentum, particularly in the U.S. and key international markets. The company reinforced its leadership in robotic focal therapy with new FDA-cleared technology and strengthened reimbursement, while reiterating 2026 core HIFU revenue guidance of $50 million to $54 million. Management outlined clear strategic priorities in commercial execution, clinical expansion, and continued innovation, supporting confidence in sustainable growth and shareholder value creation for the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Jensen Huang, Mark Zuckerberg among Trump's tech council
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has appointed some of the biggest names in business, including Meta Platforms' (META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg, Oracle's (ORCL) Executive Chairman Larry Ellison, and Nvidia's (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang, to a technology panel to advise him on science and technology.

The White House said that under Trump, the President’s Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, or PCAST, will focus on topics related to the opportunities and challenges that emerging technologies present to the American workforce and ensure all Americans thrive in the Golden Age of Innovation.

PCAST will be co-chaired by David Sacks, who has served as White House AI and crypto czar, and Michael Kratsios, another technology advisor, according to the White House's press release.

Trump appointed 13 members, which also include Advanced Micro Devices' (AMD) CEO Lisa Su and Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Co-Founder Sergey Brin.

"We’re delighted to see NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang appointed to this council and to have the opportunity to advance American leadership in AI," said Nvidia in a post on X. "America’s strength in science and technology depends on a vibrant ecosystem of researchers, developers, and industry working together to drive innovation and global leadership."

The White House said that PCAST may be composed of up to 24 members. Additional members will be appointed in the near future, the White House added.

Update: The story was updated with Nvidia's post on X.

data.title: Interactive Brokers enables crypto portfolio transfers from external wallets
data.description:
Interactive Brokers (IBKR) clients can now transfer their existing holdings in supported cryptocurrencies to their Interactive Brokers-linked crypto account, the company said on Wednesday.

The capability allows IBKR users to trade crypto at lower costs and gain access to global investment markets without selling their digital assets upfront, it added.

Interactive Brokers (IBKR) stock rose 4.9% in Wednesday morning trading.

Its clients can move supported digital assets directly from external wallets or platforms into IBKR-linked crypto accounts, enabling them to manage digital assets alongside stocks, options, futures, currencies, bonds, and other investments.

Crypto trading through Paxos or zerohash on the Interactive Brokers (IBKR) platform carries commissions of 0.12% to 0.18% of trade value with a US$1.75 minimum per order and no added spreads or markups, the company said. Many crypto platforms, however, charge trading fees of up to 2.00% of trade value or more, often with additional embedded costs.

"By enabling direct crypto portfolio transfers, we’re making it easy for traders to benefit from IBKR’s low-cost crypto trading and gain access to our full range of global markets within the same professional trading environment," said Interactive Brokers CEO Milan Galik.

data.title: MGM Resorts turns promotional in Las Vegas as it looks to boost Strip traffic
data.description:
MGM Resorts (MGM) announced on Wednesday that it is launching an all-inclusive Las Vegas Strip package that bundles a two-night stay at Luxor or Excalibur with resort fees, six meals per stay, two show tickets, two Big Apple Coaster rides, and self-parking for one vehicle. The offer starts booking immediately for stays beginning April 6, uses dynamic pricing with no blackout dates, and is sold directly through MGM channels rather than online travel agencies.

Looking to address affordability concerns, MGM (MGM) highlighted that the package is meant to give guests a simpler, upfront-priced way to combine lodging, dining, entertainment, and parking while still tapping into the variety of a typical Vegas trip.

The casino operator's move comes as Las Vegas tourism showed a soft trend in 2025, with about 3.1M fewer visitors than in the year prior. The 7.5% decline was the sharpest drop outside the pandemic since 1970, and early 2026 data showed another modest year-over-year dip even as convention attendance improved. Notably, analysts say leisure demand has weakened more than convention traffic, and midweek Strip business has been especially soft.

Shares of MGM (MGM) are up 1.8% on a year-to-date basis and stand 16% above where they stood a year ago. Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts both have a Buy rating on the casino stock.

data.title: Crude inventory rises by 6.9M barrels for week ended March 20 – EIA
data.description:
• Commercial crude stocks (excluding those in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve) for the week ended March 20: 456.2M barrels.

data.title: Sandisk stands as the best performing large-cap IT stock YTD
data.description:
U.S. large-cap information technology stocks are in a consolidation phase year-to-date, with the S&P 500 Information Technology sector down about 4% in 2026 through March after a blockbuster 24% gain in 2025. 

In this scenario, below is a list of the top 10 large-cap information technology stocks ranked by their year-to-date performance. The list includes market capitalization details and Quant ratings for each stock.

The list is topped by Sandisk Corporation (SNDK), with a YTD performance up 195.93%, followed by Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE) with gains of 117.58%. Both companies carry Strong Buy Quant ratings of 4.99. Ciena Corporation (CIEN), Western Digital Corporation (WDC), and Advanced Energy Industries, Inc. (AEIS) round out the top five performers.

The remaining stocks on the list represent diverse industries within the IT sector, from Communications Equipment to Electronic Components. Notable mentions include Corning Incorporated (GLW) with a 62.19% YTD gain and TTM Technologies, Inc. (TTMI), which carries a Strong Buy Quant rating of 4.97.

Here is the list:

  1. Sandisk Corporation (SNDK), YTD perf: 195.93%, Quant Rating: 4.99

  2. Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE), YTD perf: 117.58%, Quant Rating: 4.99

  3. Ciena Corporation (CIEN), YTD perf: 83.60%, Quant Rating: 3.49

data.title: Takeda revamps business seeking ¥200B in savings by FY28
data.description:
Takeda (TAK) announced plans to generate more than ¥200B ($1.3B) in annualized gross savings by fiscal 2028 on Wednesday, citing a business transformation exercise and a recently undertaken restructuring drive.

The Japanese drugmaker stated that its board of directors approved the changes to business practices in a bid to accelerate its product launches and improve its competitiveness and long-term growth profile in the years ahead.

"Increased efficiencies from the transformation will be achieved through streamlining of corporate functions, bringing leadership and teams closer to patients and customers, and process simplification made possible by advanced technologies," the company said.

In January, Tokyo-based Takeda (TAK) announced changes to its executive leadership team and organizational structure effective Apr. 1 in response to a fast-moving market and a complex business environment.

With ¥150B ($0.9B) in restructuring expenses in FY26, the above initiatives are anticipated to impact the company’s financial forecast for the upcoming fiscal year, as expected with its FY25 results. While there are savings in restructuring expenses in FY27 and FY28, there will be no impact on FY25 results.
